{{Short description|none}} {{Use dmy dates|date=July 2020}} {{Infobox Film Festival | name = 2006 Cannes Film Festival | image = CFF2006poster.jpg | caption = Official poster of the 59th Cannes Film Festival featuring a still from [[Wong Kar-wai]]'s 2000 film ''[[In the Mood for Love]]''.{{cite web|url=http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/archives/2006/posters.html|title=Posters 2006|work=festival-cannes.fr|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131206142527/http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/archives/2006/posters.html|archive-date=6 December 2013}} | host = [[Vincent Cassel]] | number = 20 (Main Competition) | opening = ''[[The Da Vinci Code (film)|The Da Vinci Code]]'' | closing = ''[[Transylvania (film)|Transylvania]]'' | location = [[Cannes]], France | awards = [[Palme d'Or]]:
''[[The Wind That Shakes the Barley (film)|The Wind That Shakes the Barley]]'' | date = {{Start date|2006|05|17|df=y}} – {{End date|2006|05|28|df=y}} | founded = 1946 | website = {{URL|festival-cannes.com/en}} | main = [[Cannes Film Festival]] | previous = [[2005 Cannes Film Festival|2005]] | next = [[2007 Cannes Film Festival|2007]] }} The '''59th [[Cannes Film Festival]]''' was held from 17 to 28 May 2006. Chinese filmmaker [[Wong Kar-wai]] served as jury president for the main competition, becoming the first Chinese person to do so.{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/film/2006/jan/05/news|title=Wong picked as Cannes's first Chinese president|work=[[The Guardian]]|publisher=[[Guardian Media Group|Guardian News and Media Limited]]|access-date=26 May 2017}} English filmmaker [[Ken Loach]] won the [[Palme d'Or]] for the war drama film ''[[The Wind That Shakes the Barley (film)|The Wind That Shakes the Barley]]''.{{cite web|url=http://www.cinema-francais.fr/cannes/cannes_2006.htm|title=59ème Festival de Cannes|work=cinema-francais.fr|language=fr|access-date=29 June 2017}}{{cite web|url=http://www.cannes-fest.com/cine/index.php?a=06|title=Cannes 2006 / Palmarés|work=cannes-fest.com|language=fr|access-date=29 June 2017}}{{cite news|url=https://www.foxnews.com/story/fast-facts-59th-cannes-film-festival-winners/|title=Fast Facts: 59th Cannes Film Festival Winners|publisher=[[Fox News]]|date=29 May 2006|access-date=26 May 2017}}{{cite news|url=http://www.strangecultureblog.com/2006/05/2006-cannes-film-festival-winners.html|title=2006 Cannes Film Festival Winners|work=strangecultureblog.com|access-date=26 May 2017}} The official poster for the festival features a still image from the [[Wong Kar-wai]]'s 2000 film ''[[In the Mood for Love]]'', which won the [[Cannes Film Festival Award for Best Actor|Best Actor]] award at the [[2000 Cannes Film Festival|53rd]] edition. The festival opening film was ''[[The Da Vinci Code (film)|The Da Vinci Code]]'' directed by [[Ron Howard]].{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/world/2006/jan/24/film.france|title=Da Vinci Code to open 2006 Cannes film festival|work=[[The Guardian]]|publisher=[[Guardian Media Group|Guardian News and Media Limited]]|access-date=26 May 2017}} While ''[[Transylvania (film)|Transylvania]]'' by [[Tony Gatlif]] was the closing film.{{cite news|url=http://www.urbancinefile.com.au/home/view.asp?a=11818&s=Features|title=Cannes 2006 – Preview|work=urbancinefile.com.au|access-date=26 May 2017|archive-date=14 December 2013|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131214081501/http://www.urbancinefile.com.au/home/view.asp?a=11818&s=Features|url-status=dead}} This edition also marked the first time in three years that no American film, actor, actress, or filmmaker won any awards in Cannes. [[File:Un certain regard 06.jpg|thumb|200px|2006 {{lang|fr|[[Un Certain Regard]]}} poster featuring an original drawing by Russian filmmaker [[Sergei Eisenstein]].{{cite news|url=http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/archives/2006/posters/ucr.html|title=Posters 2006|work=festival-cannes.fr|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20131214061714/http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/archives/2006/posters/ucr.html|archive-date=14 December 2013}}]] ==Juries== === Main competition === [[File:Wong Kar-wai Berlin cropped.jpg|thumb|240x240px|[[Wong Kar-wai]], 2006 Jury President]] *[[Wong Kar-wai]], Hong Kong filmmaker - Jury President{{cite web |title=All Juries 2006 |url=http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/archives/2006/allJury.html |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160304111848/http://www.festival-cannes.fr/en/archives/2006/allJury.html |archive-date=4 March 2016 |work=festival-cannes.fr}} *[[Monica Bellucci]], Italian actress *[[Helena Bonham Carter]], British actress *[[Samuel L.
